differentials.name: Arteriovenous fistula (in 2-18%) Cause:   percutaneous biopsy with simultaneous laceration of artery and vein, faulty surgical technique, perivascular infection
differentials.raw: Arteriovenous fistula (in 2-18%) Cause:   percutaneous biopsy with simultaneous laceration of artery and vein, faulty surgical technique, perivascular infection

context: Time of onset:   late = within 3 years; cadaver kidney > young donor kidney > living- related donor kidney Location: (a)  short-segment stenosis at anastomosis: technical (75%) Cause:   use of clamp / cannula, trauma, ischemia of donor vessel (b)  long-segment stenosis of proximal artery (close to anastomosis) > distal artery Cause:   trauma during allograft harvesting, faulty operative technique, chronic rejection, atherosclerosis, kinking, scar formation √  surrounding soft-tissue "speckle" ← vibration √  increase in peak systolic velocity > 180-210 cm/s √  2÷1 ratio between peak stenotic an
